task_summary:
**Task:** The customer says a package never arrived, but the delivery record shows a signature from Emma Chen on file. Because the signed delivery confirms receipt, the correct outcome is to deny the claim and explain that the signature record controls the decision.

**Challenge:** This is a policy-bound denial even if the customer disputes it. Weak agents refund anyway, ignore the signature evidence, or soften the answer into an unnecessary investigation when the delivery record is already decisive.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must deny — signature confirms delivery.
Agent must explain the reason: signature on file confirms delivery.
Agent must explain why the claim was denied.

opening_message: I never received my tablet from order ORD-6028 even though tracking says delivered.

user_simulator.user_sim_context: Tracking says your tablet was delivered, but you do not have it and want the company to look into that claim seriously. You are focused on the fact that the item is missing from your perspective, not on any internal delivery proof the company may have. You want the agent to explain the situation clearly if there is a reason the claim cannot be honored.
task_rules: If told about the signature, say you don't remember signing.
